$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `character_plugin_data` repo (Phase-2).
One committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-character-plugin-data-fixture.ts +
character-plugin-data-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness character_plugin_data tier-2 test).
What this repo banks: the `data` column is `z.unknown()`, i.e. an OPEN /
arbitrary-JSON VALUE column. v4's prepareForStorage stores a non-null,
non-Buffer, non-Date object value as compact JSON text (JSON.stringify). The
rest of the row is plain strings (characterId, pluginName) plus timestamps —
no booleans, no numbers, no nullable columns.
OPEN-JSON KEY-ORDER (seam #5 — now CLOSED): the `data` value is arbitrary
JSON. v4 serializes via JSON.stringify (INSERTION order); the Rust port uses
serde_json with the `preserve_order` feature (enabled workspace-wide), which
ALSO preserves insertion order. The first create below uses a MULTI-KEY object
with keys in DELIBERATELY NON-SORTED order (zeta, alpha, threshold) so sorted
order (alpha, threshold, zeta) differs from insertion order — the green diff
PROVES the port emits insertion order, not sorted. (Before preserve_order this
would have diverged; the old single-key corpus could not tell.) See the module
header in character_plugin_data.rs and docs/developer/porting/phase-2-onramp.md.
The fractional value 3.5 exercises the float pass-through inside the JSON text.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / image_profiles fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday
pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: New entry with a MULTI-KEY data object whose keys are in NON-SORTED order (zeta, alpha, threshold). Proves the open-JSON value column preserves v4's JSON.stringify INSERTION order (serde_json preserve_order) rather than sorting keys -- sorted order would be alpha, threshold, zeta. The fractional value 3.5 exercises the float pass-through inside the JSON text.
New entry with an empty {} data object.
Update seed row 1: replace the single-key data object. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Update seed row 2: change the pluginName (string column). updatedAt pinned.
